title: Segfault when using recursive functions
body:
Here is a golfed example from a larger description I was writing:

``` verilog
// segfault.v
module segfault(output y);
   function get_xxx;
      input i;
      begin
           get_xxx = i == 0 ? 1 : get_xxx(0);
      end
   endfunction
   assign y = get_xxx(0);
endmodule
```

As I see it, there is enough information during compilation to unwrap this function. In fact, in this case recursion does not happen at all (it should return `1` immediately without reentering `get_xxx` function).

I run it like this:

```
yosys -p 'prep' segfault.v
```

Here is the output:

```
 Yosys 0.6 (git sha1 5869d26, gcc 6.2.0-4 -O2 -fdebug-prefix-map=/build/yosys-aIBmDV/yosys-0.6=. -fstack-protector-strong -fPIC -Os)


-- Parsing `segfault.v' using frontend `verilog' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend.
Parsing Verilog input from `segfault.v' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\segfault'.
Segmentation fault
```

Am I doing something incorrectly? If so, maybe it is possible to implement a better failure mode for this case?
